   405→## 7. Validation SOP
   406→
   407→### 7.1 End-to-End Data Flow
   408→
   409→```
   410→CAPTURE --> VERIFY --> SCORE CONFIDENCE --> PUBLISH DELTA
   411→
   412→Step 1: CAPTURE (Field Agent, via PWA)
   413→  |
   414→  |  Agent selects vertical, takes photo, GPS auto-captured,
   415→  |  fills required fields (progressive disclosure)
   416→  |  GPS integrity report collected (mock location, sensors, accuracy)
   417→  |  Submission queued in IndexedDB (offline-first)
   418→  |
   419→  v
   420→Step 2: SYNC (Client --> Server)
   421→  |
   422→  |  IndexedDB queue flushes with idempotency keys
   423→  |  Exponential backoff on failure
   424→  |  Photo uploaded to Vercel Blob
   425→  |  Event written to point_events (CREATE_EVENT or ENRICH_EVENT)
   426→  |
   427→  v
   428→Step 3: VERIFY (Server-Side, Automated)
   429→  |
   430→  |  3a. GPS Validation Pipeline
   431→  |  3b. Photo Validation Pipeline
   432→  |  3c. Entity Deduplication
   433→  |  3d. Fraud Risk Scoring
   434→  |
   435→  v
   436→Step 4: SCORE CONFIDENCE
   437→  |
   438→  |  Composite confidence score computed
   439→  |  Auto-routing: approve / soft review / hard review / reject
   440→  |
   441→  v
   442→Step 5: ADMIN REVIEW (for flagged submissions)
   443→  |
   444→  |  Admin reviews photo, GPS, metadata, fraud flags
   445→  |  Approve, reject, or request re-submission
   446→  |
   447→  v
   448→Step 6: PUBLISH DELTA (Weekly)
   449→  |
   450→  |  Weekly snapshot frozen (Vercel Cron)
   451→  |  Delta computed: S(n) - S(n-1)
   452→  |  Z-score anomaly detection on delta stats
   453→  |  Published to API / dashboard / reports
   454→```
   455→
   456→### 7.2 Confidence Scoring Formula
   457→
   458→ADL uses two complementary scores:
   459→
   460→#### A. Data Quality Confidence Score (0-100)
   461→
   462→Measures how reliable a data point is. Computed per POI and updated with each event.
   463→
   464→```
   465→DATA QUALITY CONFIDENCE SCORE
   466→==============================
   467→
   468→Factor                Weight    Scoring
   469→--------------------------------------------------
   470→Recency               25%      100 if <7 days old, decays linearly to 0 at 90 days
   471→Source Count           20%      min(100, sourceCount * 25) -- more sources = higher confidence
   472→Photo Evidence         20%      100 if photo with EXIF GPS; 60 if photo without EXIF; 0 if no photo
   473→GPS Accuracy           15%      100 if <10m; 80 if <30m; 50 if <50m; 20 if <100m; 0 if >100m
   474→Reviewer Approval      10%      100 if admin approved; 50 if pending; 0 if rejected
   475→Field Completeness     10%      (filled fields / total fields) * 100
   476→
   477→FINAL SCORE = weighted sum of all factors, clamped to 0-100
   478→
   479→Publication threshold: >= 40 (below 40, data is withheld from client-facing products)
   480→High-confidence threshold: >= 70 (used for premium data products)
   481→```
   482→
   483→#### B. Fraud Risk Score (0-100)
   484→
   485→Measures how likely a submission is fraudulent. Computed at submission time.
   486→
   487→```
   488→FRAUD RISK SCORE
   489→=================
   490→
   491→Category              Weight    Signals
   492→--------------------------------------------------
   493→GPS Integrity          25%      Inverted GPS confidence (100 - GPS_CONFIDENCE_SCORE)
   494→                                Mock location detection, EXIF mismatch, IP mismatch,
   495→                                velocity analysis, sensor data
   496→
   497→Photo Integrity        25%      SHA-256 exact duplicate check (reject if different POI)
   498→                                Perceptual hash (aHash, Hamming distance <=5 = near-identical)
   499→                                EXIF fingerprint match (same device+time+GPS)
   500→                                Photo freshness (100 if <30 min, decays; <10 if >7 days)
   501→                                Screenshot detection, metadata editing detection
   502→
   503→Temporal Consistency   15%      Submission velocity (>200 km/h = block, >80 km/h = flag)
   504→                                Time-of-day plausibility
   505→                                Offline batch anomaly analysis
   506→
   507→Agent History          20%      Account age, prior fraud flags, prior rejections,
   508→                                trust tier (new/established/veteran),
   509→                                consecutive clean submissions
   510→
   511→Behavioral Patterns    15%      Self-enrichment patterns, device sharing,
   512→                                category distribution anomalies, IP reputation (VPN/datacenter)
   513→
   514→COMPOSITE FRAUD RISK = weighted sum, clamped to 0-100
   515→```
   516→
   517→#### C. Auto-Routing Rules
   518→
   519→| Fraud Risk Score | Data Quality Score | Action | XP |
   520→|---|---|---|---|
   521→| 0-20 | >= 70 | **Auto-approve** | Award immediately |
   522→| 0-20 | 40-69 | **Soft review** (admin reviews within 48h) | Escrow until review |
   523→| 21-50 | Any | **Soft review** | Escrow until review |
   524→| 51-70 | Any | **Hard review** (admin must investigate) | Deny until cleared |
   525→| 71-100 | Any | **Auto-reject** | Deny |
   526→
   527→### 7.3 Fraud Detection Checkpoints
   528→
   529→Each submission passes through these checkpoints in order:
   530→
   531→```
   532→CHECKPOINT 1: CLIENT-SIDE (before submission)
   533→  [ ] Mock location API check (Android isMocked flag)
   534→  [ ] GPS accuracy sanity (reject if accuracy = 0 or <1m on consumer device)
   535→  [ ] Sensor data collection (2 sec accelerometer sample)
   536→  [ ] Network type recording
   537→  [ ] GPS timestamp vs device clock check (flag if >30s drift)
   538→
   539→CHECKPOINT 2: SERVER-SIDE (at submission time)
   540→  [ ] EXIF GPS vs submission GPS (threshold: 1km standard, 2km low-end device)
   541→  [ ] IP geolocation vs submission GPS (threshold: 50km)
   542→  [ ] Velocity check (time + distance since last submission by same user)
   543→      - >200 km/h or >5km in <2 min = BLOCK
   544→      - >80 km/h = FLAG
   545→  [ ] Photo SHA-256 exact hash check against all prior submissions
   546→  [ ] Perceptual hash check (Hamming distance <=5 on aHash = flag)
   547→  [ ] EXIF fingerprint dedup (same device + time + GPS = flag)
   548→  [ ] Photo freshness check (>7 days old = reject; >24h = flag)
   549→  [ ] Screenshot detection (no EXIF + specific resolution patterns)
   550→  [ ] Entity proximity dedup (per-vertical thresholds, e.g., 10m for mobile money)
   551→  [ ] Geofence check (within Bonamoussadi: 4.0755-4.0999 N, 9.7185-9.7602 E)
   552→
   553→CHECKPOINT 3: BATCH ANALYSIS (daily cron)
   554→  [ ] Agent movement pattern analysis (cluster count, radius, precision distribution)
   555→  [ ] Cross-agent duplicate detection (different agents submitting same entity)
   556→  [ ] Collusion pattern detection (circular enrichment graphs)
   557→  [ ] Coordinate precision anomaly (>80% of submissions with <=4 decimal places = flag)
   558→  [ ] Alt account detection (same deviceId or IP across multiple userIds)
   559→```
   560→
   561→### 7.4 Review Workflow
   562→
   563→```
   564→ADMIN REVIEW QUEUE WORKFLOW
   565→=============================
   566→
   567→1. TRIAGE (daily, 9:00 AM WAT)
   568→   - Sort queue by fraud risk score (highest first)
   569→   - Auto-rejected items: log and archive (no action needed)
   570→   - Auto-approved items: log and publish (no action needed)
   571→   - Hard review items: investigate immediately
   572→   - Soft review items: batch review after hard reviews
   573→
   574→2. INVESTIGATION (per flagged submission)
   575→   - View side-by-side: photo | map (GPS pin) | submission data | fraud flags
   576→   - Check EXIF metadata panel (capture time, device, GPS)

    21→## Relationship to Prior Deliverables
    22→
    23→This document builds on the work of Teammates 1-6:
    24→
    25→- **Teammate 1 (Local Context):** The vertical analysis, priority matrix (Roads and Mobile Money at P0), market sizing ($4.9B alcohol market, $160M pharma market, 890 fuel stations, 100-200 mobile money agents in Bonamoussadi alone), competitor landscape, and regulatory environment form the commercial foundation. Key insight: the informal sector is ADL's biggest opportunity -- no competitor maps it systematically.
    26→- **Teammate 2 (System Design):** The delta computation engine (weekly snapshots, z-score anomaly detection, confidence scoring) is the product backbone. Without consistent deltas, there is nothing to sell. The system captures exactly the data changes that buyers care about.
    27→- **Teammate 3 (Cloud Architect):** The API design (`/api/snapshots`, `/api/deltas`, `/api/points`) defines the technical delivery mechanism for data products. The cost structure ($50-200/month infrastructure) sets the floor for pricing.
    28→- **Teammate 4 (Implementation):** The deployment pipeline and observability stack ensure reliable data delivery -- essential for enterprise customers who need SLA guarantees.
    29→- **Teammate 5 (Cybersecurity):** Compliance with Cameroon's Law No. 2024/017 (deadline June 2026) is a selling point. ADL can position as "compliant from day one" while competitors scramble.
    30→- **Teammate 6 (Anti-Fraud):** The confidence scoring and fraud detection system is the trust engine. Every data product must communicate its confidence level to justify premium pricing. Clients pay for verified ground truth, not unvalidated scrapes.
    31→
    32→**Core commercial thesis (from advisor Yannick Lefang):**
    33→- "You need consistent deltas to be able to sell, and that consistency matters."
    34→- "Africans are very transactional" -- the value proposition must be immediately clear, with tangible ROI demonstrated in the first conversation.

   234→## 2. Use Case Prioritization
   235→
   236→### Scoring Methodology
   237→
   238→Each use case is scored on 5 dimensions (1-5 scale each, 5 is best):
   239→
   240→| Criterion | Weight | 5 = Best | 1 = Worst |
   241→|---|---|---|---|

   374→## 4. Go-to-Market Strategy

   381→2. **Fastest time to sale:** Logistics companies and ride-hailing platforms have an urgent, daily operational need. No budget approval committee needed -- the Operations Manager can sign off.
   382→3. **Easiest data collection:** Roads are fixed, observable, public infrastructure. No privacy concerns. No informal/formal ambiguity. Field agents can assess conditions during regular daily routes.
   383→4. **Highest competitive moat:** No real-time road condition system exists for Douala. Google Maps does not track flooding. Waze has minimal coverage. ADL would be the only source.
   384→5. **Natural expansion:** Once logistics companies trust ADL's road data, cross-selling fuel station data (along the same routes) and mobile money agent locations is a natural extension.

   469→## 5. Competitive Positioning

   471→### 5.1 vs. Kasi Insight
   472→
   473→| Dimension | Kasi Insight | ADL | ADL Advantage |
   474→|---|---|---|---|
   475→| **Method** | Macro surveys (12,000+ monthly interviews across 20+ countries) | Ground-truth field collection with photo evidence, GPS, and confidence scoring | Kasi asks people what they think; ADL shows what actually exists on the ground |
   476→| **Granularity** | Country-level and city-level consumer insights | Neighborhood-level and street-level POI data | ADL can tell you "there are 14 informal drug vendors within 200m of Marche de Bonamoussadi" -- Kasi cannot |
   477→| **Update Frequency** | Monthly/quarterly surveys | Weekly snapshots with delta computation | ADL detects changes within days; Kasi within months |
   478→| **Coverage** | 20+ African economies | Initially Douala only (expanding) | Kasi is broad but shallow; ADL is narrow but deep |
   479→| **Informal Sector** | Surveys may reach informal actors but captures opinions, not locations | Maps the physical presence and location of every informal business | ADL is the only source for geolocated informal sector data |
   480→| **Pricing** | Enterprise pricing (estimated $20K-100K/year) | Accessible tiers starting at CFA 200K/month (~$325) | ADL is more accessible for local and regional companies |
   481→
   482→**Positioning statement vs. Kasi:** "Kasi Insight tells you what African consumers think. ADL shows you what exists on the ground. We're complementary, not competitive -- but if you need to know where to distribute your product, you need a map, not a survey."

   486→| Dimension | Nielsen / Kantar | ADL | ADL Advantage |
   487→|---|---|---|---|

   489→| **Cost** | $50K-500K+ per study; enterprise subscriptions $100K+/year | Starting at CFA 200K/month (~$325/month) | 10-100x cheaper for comparable geographic coverage |
   490→| **Frequency** | Monthly or quarterly retail audits | Weekly snapshots | 4-12x more frequent |
   491→| **Informal Sector** | Largely excluded from panels (hard to recruit, high churn) | Core focus -- maps both formal and informal | ADL captures the 30-50% of economy that Nielsen/Kantar miss |
   492→| **Cameroon Coverage** | Limited; Cameroon is a small market for global firms | Cameroon-first, ground-up | Nielsen/Kantar's Africa efforts focus on Nigeria, Kenya, South Africa; Cameroon is underserved |
   493→| **Local Knowledge** | Remote/outsourced field teams | Local field agents with community knowledge | Better contextual understanding, better access to informal sector |
   494→
   495→**Positioning statement vs. Nielsen/Kantar:** "Nielsen and Kantar give you a quarterly sample of the formal economy for $100K. ADL gives you a weekly census of the entire economy -- formal and informal -- for a fraction of the cost. And we're actually on the ground in Douala."

   635→### 7.1 Which Verticals Generate Disproportionate Value
   636→
   637→**The top 20% of effort that produces 80% of commercial value:**
   638→
   639→| Rank | Vertical | % of Data Collection Effort | % of Estimated Revenue | Revenue/Effort Ratio | Verdict |
   640→|---|---|---|---|---|---|
   641→| 1 | **Mobile Money** | 15% (visible, daytime, fixed signage) | 30% (telcos have massive budgets) | **2.0x** | **Highest ROI** |
   642→| 2 | **Transport Roads** | 10% (observable, public, during regular routes) | 25% (logistics, ride-hailing, development) | **2.5x** | **Highest ROI** |
   643→| 3 | **Fuel** | 5% (few stations, easy to visit) | 10% (fuel companies + logistics cross-sell) | **2.0x** | **High ROI** |
   644→| 4 | **Alcohol** | 25% (high informal density, night operations, difficult classification) | 20% (breweries have budget but collection is hard) | **0.8x** | **Medium ROI** |
   645→| 5 | **Billboards** | 10% (fixed, visible, daytime) | 8% (smaller market, niche buyers) | **0.8x** | **Medium ROI** |
   646→| 6 | **Pharmacy** | 15% (informal vendors mobile, formal pharmacies easy) | 5% (pharma companies + public health projects) | **0.3x** | **Low ROI short-term, high project value** |
   647→| 7 | **Census** | 20% (requires systematic building-by-building coverage) | 2% (slow-burn, hard to monetize quickly) | **0.1x** | **Strategic long-term value** |
   648→
   649→**Conclusion:** Focus 30% of collection resources on Mobile Money + Transport Roads to capture 55% of estimated revenue. Add Fuel (5% effort) for 65% total. These three verticals are the 80/20 sweet spot.

   750→## Summary: The Commercial Thesis
   751→
   752→ADL's commercial value is built on three pillars:
   753→
   754→1. **Ground truth that nobody else has.** No competitor maps the informal economy at street level with verified, GPS-located, photo-evidenced data points. In a country where 30-50% of GDP is informal, this is an enormous gap.
   755→
   756→2. **Consistent deltas that demonstrate change.** As Yannick Lefang said, "You need consistent deltas to be able to sell." Weekly snapshots with computed deltas turn static maps into dynamic intelligence. The delta is the product -- not the map.
   757→
   758→3. **Immediately clear value for transactional buyers.** "Africans are very transactional." Every data product must answer: "If I buy this, what money do I save or make this week?" For logistics companies: "You avoid a CFA 200K truck reroute." For telcos: "You identify 15 agent gaps before your competitor fills them." For breweries: "You find 23 bars you're not selling to." The value must be concrete, immediate, and measurable.
   759→
   760→**First revenue target:** CFA 5-10M/month by month 6 (2-3 paying customers at Professional tier). **12-month target:** CFA 20-40M/month (1-2 enterprise customers + 5-8 professional tier). **Path to CFA 100M+/month:** City-wide Douala coverage across all verticals, with enterprise contracts from telcos and development organizations.

   895→## 8. Go/No-Go Decision Framework
   896→
   897→### 8.1 Decision Date: W6D4 (May 21, 2026)
   898→
   899→The Go/No-Go decision determines whether ADL proceeds to Phase 2 (expanded collection, first paid customers) or pauses/pivots.
   900→
   901→### 8.2 Minimum Success Criteria for "GO"
   902→
   903→ALL of the following must be met to proceed:
   904→
   905→| # | Criterion | Threshold | Rationale |
   906→|---|---|---|---|
   907→| 1 | **Total POIs captured** | >= 500 | Minimum dataset size to demonstrate value across verticals |
   908→| 2 | **P0 vertical coverage** (Roads + MoMo) | >= 70% of estimated POIs | Core product verticals must be substantially complete |
   909→| 3 | **Audit accuracy** | >= 80% | Data must be trustworthy; below 80% = unreliable product |
   910→| 4 | **Deltas computed** | >= 3 weekly deltas (D1, D2, D3) | Must prove the system can track change over time |
   911→| 5 | **Agent team functional** | >= 2 agents active at pilot end | Must have retained enough team to continue operations |
   912→| 6 | **Client outreach initiated** | >= 5 prospects contacted | Must have started commercial validation |
   913→| 7 | **Platform stability** | Zero data loss incidents | Data integrity is non-negotiable for a data company |

# ADL 6-Week Pilot Plan -- Bonamoussadi, Douala

**Purpose:** Operational field guide for executing the Bonamoussadi pilot from April 14 to May 22, 2026. Covers team structure, budget, KPIs, weekly milestones, and Go/No-Go decision framework. This document is self-contained and designed for day-to-day use by the CEO and Field Team Lead.

**Pilot Dates:** April 14 -- May 22, 2026 (6 weeks, 30 working days)
**Pilot Area:** Bonamoussadi, Douala (geofence: 4.0755--4.0999 N, 9.7185--9.7602 E)
**Verticals:** billboard, alcohol_outlet, census_proxy, fuel_station, mobile_money, pharmacy, transport_road

---

## 1. Team Structure

| # | Role | Name / Status | Location | Monthly Compensation (CFA) | Key Responsibilities |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **CEO / Technical Lead** | Charles Mahouve | Lyon, France (remote) | -- | Platform development, admin review queue, client outreach, Go/No-Go decision, budget oversight |
| 2 | **Field Team Lead** | To be hired by W1D3 | Douala, Bonamoussadi | 200,000 | Agent recruitment and training, daily supervision, spot-check audits, zone management, daily standup facilitation |
| 3 | **Field Agent 1** | To be hired by W1D4 | Douala, Bonamoussadi | 100,000 base + 20,000 transport + up to 30,000 bonus | 30--50 POI submissions/day, 8 AM--5 PM field hours, photo + GPS for every submission |
| 4 | **Field Agent 2** | To be hired by W1D4 | Douala, Bonamoussadi | 100,000 base + 20,000 transport + up to 30,000 bonus | Same as Agent 1 |
| 5 | **Field Agent 3** | To be hired by W1D4 | Douala, Bonamoussadi | 100,000 base + 20,000 transport + up to 30,000 bonus | Same as Agent 1 |

**Hiring criteria for Field Team Lead:** University-educated (GIS, data, IT, or business preferred); fluent French + functional English; smartphone-literate; local knowledge of Bonamoussadi; leadership experience (managed 2+ people); available full-time for 6 weeks; references required.

**Hiring criteria for Field Agents:** Age 20--35; secondary school minimum; smartphone-literate; knows Bonamoussadi geography; physically able to walk 8--15 km/day; available full-time for 6 weeks; no criminal record; community references.

**Zone Assignments:**
- Agent 1: Zone A (north Bonamoussadi) + Zone D rotation
- Agent 2: Zone B (central / Carrefour Bonamoussadi)
- Agent 3: Zone C (south / Makepe)

**Decision Date:** W6D4 (May 21, 2026)
**Decision Maker:** Charles Mahouve (CEO), with Team Lead input and advisor consultation (Yannick Lefang)

### 6.1 Minimum Success Criteria

ALL 7 criteria must be met for a GO outcome:

| # | Criterion | Minimum Threshold | How Measured |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Total POIs captured | >= 500 | Count of unique CREATE_EVENT records |
| 2 | P0 vertical coverage (transport_road + mobile_money) | >= 70% of estimated POIs | Captured vs. Teammate 1 estimates |
| 3 | Audit accuracy | >= 80% | Spot-check verified POIs matching reality / total audited |
| 4 | Weekly deltas computed | >= 3 (D1, D2, D3) | Computed deltas in database |
| 5 | Agent team functional | >= 2 agents active at pilot end | Active agents on final day |
| 6 | Client outreach initiated | >= 5 prospects contacted | Outreach log count |
| 7 | Platform stability | Zero data loss incidents | Incident log |

### 6.2 Decision Outcomes

|---|---|---|

| **STOP** | < 5 met OR accuracy < 70% OR data loss incident OR zero commercial interest | Stop pilot; document lessons learned; reassess product-market fit; consider different geography or vertical focus |

# ADL Risk Register -- Bonamoussadi Pilot

**Purpose:** Operational risk tracker for the 6-week Bonamoussadi pilot (April 14 -- May 22, 2026). Contains a risk heat map, categorized risk table with mitigations, and a weekly review checklist. Update the Status column at every Friday review meeting.

**Risk Scoring:** Probability (1--5) x Impact (1--5) = Score (1--25)
- Probability: 1 = Very Low, 2 = Low, 3 = Medium, 4 = High, 5 = Very High
- Impact: 1 = Negligible, 2 = Low, 3 = Medium, 4 = High, 5 = Critical

## 2. Risk Register

### 2.1 Fraud Risks

| ID | Risk | Prob | Impact | Score | Mitigation | Owner | Trigger | Escalation | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| R01 | **GPS spoofing by agents** -- fabricated submissions from home using mock location apps | 4-High | 5-Critical | **20** | Multi-layered detection: client-side mock location API check, EXIF GPS cross-validation (1km threshold, 2km for low-end devices), IP geolocation (50km), velocity analysis (>200km/h = block, >80km/h = flag), accelerometer sensor data, batch statistical anomaly detection | Charles | >5% of submissions flagged for GPS anomalies in a single week; any agent with >3 GPS flags in one day | Immediate: suspend agent pending investigation; review all agent submissions for the day; escalate to Team Lead for field verification |
| R02 | **Fabricated / ghost entity submissions** -- phantom POIs with random storefront photos | 4-High | 5-Critical | **20** | Photo evidence required for all submissions; perceptual hash (aHash) deduplication (Hamming distance <=5 = flag); entity proximity thresholds per vertical (e.g., 10m for mobile_money); spot-check audits (10--50 POIs/week); XP escrowed until admin review | Charles | Audit reveals >20% of sampled POIs do not exist at stated location; photo hashing flags >3 near-identical photos from same agent | Immediate: halt agent submissions; conduct field verification of all agent's recent POIs; if confirmed, terminate agent |
| R10 | **Recycled / stock photos** -- reusing one trip's photos for multiple submissions | 4-High | 4-High | **16** | SHA-256 exact hash matching; perceptual hash with Hamming distance threshold; EXIF fingerprint matching (same device + time + GPS = flag); screenshot detection (no EXIF + specific resolution patterns) | Charles | Photo hash system flags >2 near-identical photos from same agent in one day | Review agent's submission batch; if confirmed recycling, reject all and issue warning; second offense = termination |
| R11 | **Agent collusion to inflate XP** -- circular enrichment between agents | 3-Medium | 4-High | **12** | Graph analysis of user-to-user and user-to-point relationships; detect circular enrichment patterns (Agent A creates, Agent B enriches, reverse); monitor cross-agent duplicate submissions within proximity thresholds | Charles | >5 instances of Agent X enriching Agent Y's POIs AND Agent Y enriching Agent X's POIs in one week | Freeze XP for involved agents; conduct field audit of the specific POIs; if confirmed, restructure zone assignments to prevent contact |
| R14 | **Alt account farming** -- agents registering multiple accounts to multiply XP | 3-Medium | 4-High | **12** | Device fingerprint binding (deviceId in localStorage); same IP + device fingerprint across multiple userIds = flag; phone verification requirement; limit one account per SIM | Charles | DeviceId or IP appears linked to >1 active user account | Suspend secondary accounts immediately; investigate primary account; if confirmed, terminate all accounts for that individual |
| R12 | **Fraud detection false positives frustrate agents** -- legitimate submissions wrongly flagged | 3-Medium | 3-Medium | **9** | Low-end device exceptions for GPS accuracy thresholds (2km EXIF vs 1km standard); gradual threshold tuning based on W2 data; transparent quality feedback to agents; trust tiers (new/established/veteran) with escalating autonomy | Charles | Agent complaints about false flags exceed 3 per week; agent morale visibly declining due to rejections | Adjust thresholds based on device-specific analysis; communicate changes to agents; consider promoting high-trust agents to "established" tier earlier |

## 3. Weekly Risk Review Checklist

Complete every Friday during the weekly review meeting (4:00 PM WAT). The Team Lead and CEO review each question together.

### Friday Risk Review Questions

- [ ] **1. Fraud signals:** Did any agent trigger >3 fraud flags this week? Are there new patterns (GPS clustering, photo reuse, velocity anomalies) that suggest systematic fraud?

- [ ] **2. Data quality trend:** Is the submission quality score (GPS <50m + photo + required fields) trending up, stable, or down compared to last week? If down, which agent or vertical is responsible?

- [ ] **3. Agent health:** Are all agents active and meeting productivity targets (30--50 submissions/day)? Has any agent expressed intent to leave, shown declining motivation, or missed days?

- [ ] **4. Technical stability:** Were there any sync failures >5%, platform outages, data loss events, or database alerts this week? Is the review backlog under 100?

- [ ] **5. Budget tracking:** What is cumulative spend vs. plan? Are we within the 15% contingency, or approaching the ceiling? Any unexpected costs?

- [ ] **6. New or escalated risks:** Has any risk materialized that was not on this register? Has any existing risk changed in probability or impact since last week? Update the Status column for any changed risks.

### After the Review

- Update risk Status column (Open / Mitigated / Materialized / Closed)
- Log any new risks with ID, scoring, and mitigation
